varieties.name: Standard Red Morphotype 🏆
varieties.traits: Small to medium evergreen shrub, 1.5-3m cultivated height, glossy dark green elongated leaves in spiral clusters, slow-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high, multiple flushes year-round in Brisbane, peak fruiting summer/autumn
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent Brisbane adaptation, tolerates 0°C when established in protected position, requires consistent moisture and acidic mulch, fruiting after 3-4 years, suitable for containers
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery (QLD), Herbalistics (NSW), All Rare Herbs (Australia-wide), Plant Haven Nursery

varieties.name: 'Imperial' Morphotype
varieties.origin: Florida selection from West African stock
varieties.traits: Medium-sized plant, slightly larger than standard, more vigorous growth habit
varieties.yield_notes: Highest yielding morphotype - 2.76 kg/tree/year in Florida trials, 1,800-1,900 fruits/tree/season
varieties.growing_notes: Top commercial choice for Brisbane subtropical conditions, excellent heat tolerance, consistent heavy producer, good humidity resistance, requires acidic soil and regular fertilization
varieties.availability: Specialist tropical fruit nurseries Queensland, imported Florida stock occasionally available

varieties.name: 'Cardinal' Morphotype
varieties.traits: Distinctive larger leaves, robust plant structure
varieties.yield_notes: High fruit count 1,800-1,900 fruits/season, moderate total weight
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent subtropical performance, attractive ornamental value, elongated fruit preferred for culinary presentation, Brisbane-tested with good results
varieties.availability: Limited availability through specialty nurseries, Queensland rare fruit collectors

varieties.name: 'Cherry' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for Brisbane container culture, superior pulp thickness means higher miraculin content per fruit, excellent for home gardeners with limited space
varieties.availability: Rare in Australia, worth seeking from specialty collectors

varieties.name: 'Vermilion' Morphotype
varieties.traits: Medium plant size, attractive glossy foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Good subtropical adaptation, attractive fruit color for ornamental value, reliable producer in protected Brisbane gardens

varieties.name: 'Scarlet' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited to Brisbane humidity, compact size ideal for smaller gardens, reliable subtropical performer

varieties.name: 'Flame' Morphotype
varieties.traits: Upright growth habit, slightly smaller stature
varieties.growing_notes: Premium choice for miraculin content - highest sweetening power per berry, excellent subtropical tolerance, worth seeking for maximum taste-modifying effect
varieties.availability: Rare, highly sought after by collectors

varieties.name: 'Sangria' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable Brisbane subtropical climate, attractive darker fruit color, reliable but not highest producing

varieties.name: 'Holly' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Lower miraculin content (0.07 mg/g - lowest recorded), interesting ornamental but not recommended for miraculin production, suitable Brisbane climate

varieties.name: 'Wine' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Unique dark berry color, suitable subtropical adaptation, ornamental value

varieties.name: Giant Miracle Fruit (Synsepalum subcordatum)
varieties.traits: Large-leaf variety, grows into small tree 6-9m, much larger than S. dulcificum, faster early growth
varieties.yield_notes: More prolific in early years, yields equalize with age
varieties.growing_notes: WARNING: Does NOT contain miraculin - fruit lacks taste-modifying properties. Often confused with true Miracle Fruit. Suitable Brisbane subtropical climate as ornamental, not for miraculin production
varieties.availability: Top Tropicals, specialty collectors - verify species before purchase

varieties.name: Standard Red Morphotype 🏆
varieties.origin: West Africa (traditional cultivation, introduced to India 1960s)
varieties.traits: Evergreen shrub 2-4m in Kerala tropical conditions, glossy foliage, slow initial growth then moderate
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent in Kerala - year-round fruiting with peak crops May-September, produces hundreds of berries
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect Kerala adaptation, loves monsoon humidity, excellent heat tolerance 30-35°C, thrives in partial shade, requires acidic soil management in Kerala's varied soils, fruiting begins year 3-4
varieties.availability: Greens of Kerala (Kottayam), Cheerakuzhy Nurseries (Palakkad), Homegrown Biotech (Kanjirappally), Golden Hills Farm, Kerala Nursery, Exotic Flora (Kadiyam)

varieties.name: Yellow-Fruited Morphotype 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Southeast Nigeria (Igbo regions) - possibly center of origin for this morphotype, traditional cultivation
varieties.traits: Similar growth habit to red morphotype, shrub 2-4m
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, larger individual fruits increase total harvest weight

varieties.name: West African Traditional Large-Fruit Type 🏆 🌱
varieties.origin: Ghana, Benin traditional farms - indigenous cultivation
varieties.traits: Robust shrub, traditional West African germplasm
varieties.yield_notes: Variable but traditional favorite for size

varieties.name: 'Imperial' Morphotype
varieties.traits: Vigorous grower in tropical conditions, larger plant
varieties.growing_notes: Top commercial choice for Kerala tropical conditions, loves heat and humidity, excellent monsoon tolerance, consistent heavy production year-round, ideal for commercial plantations
varieties.availability: Occasionally available through progressive Kerala nurseries importing Florida genetics

varieties.name: 'Cardinal' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent Kerala monsoon adaptation, attractive landscape plant, heat-loving, suitable for Kerala home gardens

varieties.name: 'Flame' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: PREMIUM Kerala choice for maximum miraculin potency, smaller berry = more concentrated sweetening power, excellent tropical adaptation, sought after by commercial processors

varieties.name: 'Vermilion' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Good Kerala monsoon tolerance, reliable heat-loving producer

varieties.name: 'Scarlet' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent Kerala humidity adaptation, thrives in tropical heat

varieties.name: 'Sangria' Morphotype
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable Kerala tropical climate, attractive dark berries

varieties.name: Giant Miracle Fruit (Synsepalum subcordatum)
varieties.growing_notes: WARNING: NO taste-modifying properties despite name. Ornamental only. Perfect Kerala tropical growth. Verify species before purchase.
varieties.availability: Occasionally available Kerala nurseries - confirm true species
